seoTitle: Town Common vs Townson — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Town Common and Townson. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Town Common vs Townson: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Town Common and Townson.
heading: Town Common vs Townson — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Town Common, Queensland and Townson, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Town Common has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Townson (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Town Common or Townson safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Town Common has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Townson scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Town Common scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. Townson scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Town Common or Townson?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Town Common has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
